---
title: "Week 1: Basics in R"
subtitle: "Practice Reading and Manipulating Data"
author: David Titus
date: 2026-06-24
image: "https://github.com/bhelsel/2026-RLAB-Practical-Exercise---
title: "Practical Exercise Template"
subtitle: "Adjust the YAML each week when doing a new exercise"
author: David Titus
date: 2026-06-24
image: "https://github.com/bhelsel/2026-RLAB-Practical-Exercises/raw/main/images/rlab-placeholder.png"
execute: 
  echo: true
  eval: true
  message: false
  warning: false
---

**Assignment**. Add assignment details here. Update this section with the
current week's tasks or instructions, and any additional resources to complete
the assignment.

## Introduction to dplyr

```{r}

library(dplyr)

starwars |>
  mutate(name, bmi = mass / ((height / 100)^2)) |>
  select(name:mass, bmi)

```

## Introduction to ggplot2

```{r}

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy, colour = class)) +
  geom_point()

```s/raw/main/images/rlab-placeholder.png"
execute: 
  echo: true
  eval: true
  message: false
  warning: false
---

**Assignment**. Practice for Dr. Julianne Clina's introduction to R presentation. 
This Quarto document will be used to practice my first GitHub pull request.

## Hello R-LAB

```{r}

message("My first GitHub push worked!")
x <- 1:10
mean(x)library(dplyr)


```
